[PUBLISHED DATE]: 2020
[TITLE]: Highland Tavern becomes 'almost daily' source of complaints, awaits possible liquor permit suspension
[METADATA/INTRO]: Highland Tavern becomes ‘almost daily’ source of complaints, awaits possible liquor permit suspension After receiving several citations for breaking COVID-19 health orders, an Akron bar is waiting to see if it will be fined or have its liquor license suspended...
[BEST EVIDENCE CHUNK]: He said bar owners in general don’t understand the orders' intent. Gutentag said bar owners wonder why a beer is drunk at 10:59 p.m. different from one consumed at 11:01 p.m., and what makes bars more dangerous than other businesses. 
 Rule 80, which sets last call at 10 p.m. and the consumption deadline at 11 p.m., is hurting bar and restaurant owners, he said, and making it hard for them to stay afloat while also paying rent, taxes and inventory. 
 He said those losses are trickling down to the staff as well. 
 Gutentag said everyone should do their best to stay safe, but that people standing near each other in a retail store and people near each other in a bar are not treated the same way.

[PUBLISHED DATE]: 2020-10-26
[TITLE]: Wales lockdown confusion after Tesco tweet claims it cannot sell period products | Wales | The Guardian
[METADATA/INTRO]: Tesco has apologised after a customer was told that period products were not being sold in its Welsh stores because of the devolved administration’s ban on selling non-essential items during the firebreak lockdown. There has been continued confusion and controversy...
[BEST EVIDENCE CHUNK]: “If there are anomalies, we will look at whether the guidance needs to be revised or strengthened to make it clear that supermarkets have some discretion to sell to people who are in genuine need,” Gething said. 
 His announcement came after a tweet sent from Tesco’s official account told a customer, who said a store had refused to sell them period pads, that it was government policy that these items not be sold in its stores. 
 Tesco has since deleted the tweet and published an apology, saying: “Of course sanitary products are essential items and are available to customers in all of our stores, including those in Wales. The reply to this customer was sent by mistake and we’re very sorry for any confusion caused.”
